126:     ///   5. state.repo (global config)
127:     ///   6. None (let gh decide)
128:     pub fn effective_pr_repo(&self, branch: &str) -> Option<String> {
129:         self.effective_pr_repo_inner(branch, 0)
130:     }
131: 
132:     fn effective_pr_repo_inner(&self, branch: &str, depth: usize) -> Option<String> {
133:         // Guard against cycles in stack metadata.
134:         if depth > 50 {
135:             return None;
136:         }
137:         // 1. Per-branch override
138:         if let Some(meta) = self.branches.get(branch) {
139:             if let Some(ref r) = meta.pr_repo {
140:                 if !r.is_empty() {
141:                     return Some(r.clone());
142:                 }
143:             }
144:             // 2. Parent's push remote → repo from URL.
145:             // The parent branch was pushed to some remote; that remote's repo is where
146:             // this branch's PR should be created (because the base ref exists there).
147:             if !self.is_trunk(&meta.parent) {
148:                 let parent_remote = self.effective_push_remote_inner(&meta.parent, depth + 1);
149:                 if let Some(repo) = Self::repo_from_remote(&parent_remote) {
150:                     return Some(repo);
151:                 }
152:             }
153:         }
154:         // 3. Git tracking remote for this branch → repo from URL
155:         if let Some(remote_name) = git::tracking_remote(branch) {
156:             if let Some(repo) = Self::repo_from_remote(&remote_name) {
157:                 return Some(repo);
158:             }
159:         }
160:         // 4. Default branch's tracking remote → repo from URL
161:         if let Ok(default_branch) = git::default_branch() {
162:             if let Some(remote_name) = git::tracking_remote(&default_branch) {
163:                 if let Some(repo) = Self::repo_from_remote(&remote_name) {
164:                     return Some(repo);
165:                 }
166:             }
167:         }
168:         // 5. Global state.repo
169:         self.repo.clone().filter(|s| !s.is_empty())
170:     }
171: 
172:     /// Extract owner/repo from a git remote's URL.
173:     fn repo_from_remote(remote_name: &str) -> Option<String> {
174:         let url = git::remote_url(remote_name).ok()?;
175:         crate::github::repo_name_from_url(&url)
176:     }

180:     }
181:

184:     }
